summary: Audience-sourced recording captures a high‑energy 1981 show that cooks from start to finish, highlighted by a scorching “Eyes of the World”, fiery jam into drums and a hot Around & Around.
review: The concert is repeatedly described as “smoking” with tremendous energy, crisp Jerry tones and flawless jams into the drum segment.  While most fans rave about the performance – especially Eyes of the World, the Drums jam and the scorching Around & Around segue – one reviewer calls it solid but not spectacular, keeping the overall sentiment upbeat.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise (70% weight) outweighs a few lukewarm comments; recording is noted as good with clear audience sound, leading to an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers mention crisp Jerry tone and “good sound”; no major complaints about muddiness or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with a hot Bob Weir segue into Around & Around, an inspired jam into Drums, and generally keeps the crowd energized throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for crisp tone and “crispy Jerry” throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s segue is called a “hump”, noted as a scorching hot jam into Around & Around.
band_member_comments.Drums: Jam into the Drum segment described as flawless and amazing.
